title: senna pneumatica
intro: Senna pneumatica, also known as the desert senna, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the legume family and is native to the deserts of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of senna pneumatica lie in its seeds, which contain anthraquinone glycosides that stimulate bowel movements and have been used to treat constipation and other gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of consuming senna pneumatica include relief from constipation, improved digestion, and reduced risk of certain gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the seeds of senna pneumatica are often dried, ground, and used as a laxative or combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, or capsules that promote digestive health.
